title: poa nemoralis subsp. feratiana
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. feratiana, also known as Alpine Fescue, is a subspecies of the Poa nemoralis grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alpine Fescue include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Alpine Fescue can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
